search_text: Semantic-Guided Generative Image Augmentation Method with Diffusion Models for Image Classification 用于图像分类的具有扩散模型的语义引导生成图像增强方法 Existing image augmentation methods consist of two categories: perturbation-based methods and generative methods. Perturbation-based methods apply pre-defined perturbations to augment an original image, but only locally vary the image, thus lacking image diversity. In contrast, generative methods bring more image diversity in the augmented images but may not preserve semantic consistency, thus incorrectly changing the essential semantics of the original image. To balance image diversity and semantic consistency in augmented images, we propose SGID, a Semantic-guided Generative Image augmentation method with Diffusion models for image classification. Specifically, SGID employs diffusion models to generate augmented images with good image diversity. More importantly, SGID takes image labels and captions as guidance to maintain semantic consistency between the augmented and original images. Experimental results show that SGID outperforms the best augmentation baseline by 1.72% on ResNet-50 (from scratch), 0.33% on ViT (ImageNet-21k), and 0.14% on CLIP-ViT (LAION-2B). Moreover, SGID can be combined with other image augmentation baselines and further improves the overall performance. We demonstrate the semantic consistency and image diversity of SGID through quantitative human and automated evaluations, as well as qualitative case studies. 现有的图像增强方法包括两类：基于扰动的方法和生成方法。基于扰动的方法应用预定义的扰动来增强原始图像，但仅局部改变图像，因此缺乏图像多样性。相比之下，生成方法在增强图像中带来了更多的图像多样性，但可能无法保持语义一致性，从而错误地改变了原始图像的基本语义。为了平衡增强图像中的图像多样性和语义一致性，我们提出了 SGID，一种语义引导的生成图像增强方法，具有用于图像分类的扩散模型。具体来说，SGID 采用扩散模型来生成具有良好图像多样性的增强图像。更重要的是，SGID 以图像标签和标题作为指导，以保持增强图像和原始图像之间的语义一致性。实验结果表明，SGID 在 ResNet-50（从头开始）上优于最佳增强基线 1.72%，在 ViT (ImageNet-21k) 上优于最佳增强基线 0.33%，在 CLIP-ViT (LAION-2B) 上优于最佳增强基线 0.14%。此外，SGID可以与其他图像增强基线相结合，进一步提高整体性能。我们通过定量的人工和自动评估以及定性案例研究来证明 SGID 的语义一致性和图像多样性。 本文提出一种基于扩散模型的语义引导生成图像增强方法SGID，旨在提升图像分类任务中增强图像的多样性与语义一致性。 Bohan Li,Xiao Xu,Xinghao Wang,Yutai Hou,Yunlong Feng,Feng Wang,Xuanliang Zhang,Qingfu Zhu,Wanxiang Che cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Guy Tennenholtz,Nadav Merlis,Lior Shani,Martin Mladenov,Craig Boutilier
title: Reinforcement Learning with History-Dependent Dynamic Contexts
title_zh: 具有历史相关动态上下文的强化学习
abstract: We introduce Dynamic Contextual Markov Decision Processes (DCMDPs), a novel reinforcement learning framework for history-dependent environments that generalizes the contextual MDP framework to handle non-Markov environments, where contexts change over time. We consider special cases of the model, with a focus on logistic DCMDPs, which break the exponential dependence on history length by leveraging aggregation functions to determine context transitions. This special structure allows us to derive an upper-confidence-bound style algorithm for which we establish regret bounds. Motivated by our theoretical results, we introduce a practical model-based algorithm for logistic DCMDPs that plans in a latent space and uses optimism over history-dependent features. We demonstrate the efficacy of our approach on a recommendation task (using MovieLens data) where user behavior dynamics evolve in response to recommendations.
summary: We introduce Dynamic Contextual Markov Decision Processes (DCMDPs), a novel reinforcement learning framework for history-dependent environments that generalizes the contextual MDP framework to handle non-Markov environments, where contexts change over time. We consider special cases of the model, with a focus on logistic DCMDPs, which break the exponential dependence on history length by leveraging aggregation functions to determine context transitions. This special structure allows us to derive an upper-confidence-bound style algorithm for which we establish regret bounds. Motivated by our theoretical results, we introduce a practical model-based algorithm for logistic DCMDPs that plans in a latent space and uses optimism over history-dependent features. We demonstrate the efficacy of our approach on a recommendation task (using MovieLens data) where user behavior dynamics evolve in response to recommendations.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了动态上下文马尔可夫决策过程 (DCMDP)，这是一种针对历史相关环境的新型强化学习框架，它概括了上下文 MDP 框架以处理上下文随时间变化的非马尔可夫环境。我们考虑模型的特殊情况，重点关注逻辑 DCMDP，它通过利用聚合函数来确定上下文转换，打破了对历史长度的指数依赖性。这种特殊的结构使我们能够导出置信上限风格的算法，并为其建立遗憾界限。在我们的理论结果的推动下，我们引入了一种基于模型的实用逻辑 DCMDP 算法，该算法在潜在空间中进行规划，并使用对历史相关特征的乐观态度。我们展示了我们的方法在推荐任务（使用 MovieLens 数据）上的有效性，其中用户行为动态随推荐而变化。

AI.tldr: 本文提出动态上下文马尔可夫决策过程（DCMDPs）框架，解决历史依赖的非马尔可夫强化学习问题，并设计了基于聚合函数的算法与实际应用验证。
AI.motivation: 传统上下文MDP无法处理上下文随时间变化的非马尔可夫环境，而实际问题（如推荐系统）中用户行为动态演化需考虑历史依赖。
AI.method: 引入DCMDPs框架，通过聚合函数构建逻辑DCMDPs以降低历史长度依赖，设计基于潜在空间规划的模型算法，结合历史特征的乐观策略。
AI.result: 理论推导出遗憾界，基于MovieLens数据集的推荐任务验证了算法在动态上下文环境中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: DCMDPs框架及算法有效处理历史依赖动态环境，为实际应用（如推荐系统）提供了理论支持与实用方案。

authors_text: Haoru Tan,Sitong Wu,Jimin Pi
title: Semantic Diffusion Network for Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 用于语义分割的语义扩散网络
abstract: Precise and accurate predictions over boundary areas are essential for semantic segmentation. However, the commonly-used convolutional operators tend to smooth and blur local detail cues, making it difficult for deep models to generate accurate boundary predictions. In this paper, we introduce an operator-level approach to enhance semantic boundary awareness, so as to improve the prediction of the deep semantic segmentation model. Specifically, we first formulate the boundary feature enhancement as an anisotropic diffusion process. We then propose a novel learnable approach called semantic diffusion network (SDN) to approximate the diffusion process, which contains a parameterized semantic difference convolution operator followed by a feature fusion module. Our SDN aims to construct a differentiable mapping from the original feature to the inter-class boundary-enhanced feature. The proposed SDN is an efficient and flexible module that can be easily plugged into existing encoder-decoder segmentation models. Extensive experiments show that our approach can achieve consistent improvements over several typical and state-of-the-art segmentation baseline models on challenging public benchmarks. The code will be released soon.
summary: Precise and accurate predictions over boundary areas are essential for semantic segmentation. However, the commonly-used convolutional operators tend to smooth and blur local detail cues, making it difficult for deep models to generate accurate boundary predictions. In this paper, we introduce an operator-level approach to enhance semantic boundary awareness, so as to improve the prediction of the deep semantic segmentation model. Specifically, we first formulate the boundary feature enhancement as an anisotropic diffusion process. We then propose a novel learnable approach called semantic diffusion network (SDN) to approximate the diffusion process, which contains a parameterized semantic difference convolution operator followed by a feature fusion module. Our SDN aims to construct a differentiable mapping from the original feature to the inter-class boundary-enhanced feature. The proposed SDN is an efficient and flexible module that can be easily plugged into existing encoder-decoder segmentation models. Extensive experiments show that our approach can achieve consistent improvements over several typical and state-of-the-art segmentation baseline models on challenging public benchmarks. The code will be released soon.
abstract_zh: 对边界区域的精确预测对于语义分割至关重要。然而，常用的卷积算子往往会平滑和模糊局部细节线索，使得深度模型难以生成准确的边界预测。在本文中，我们引入了一种算子级方法来增强语义边界感知，从而提高深度语义分割模型的预测。具体来说，我们首先将边界特征增强表示为各向异性扩散过程。然后，我们提出了一种称为语义扩散网络（SDN）的新型可学习方法来近似扩散过程，其中包含参数化语义差异卷积算子，后跟特征融合模块。我们的SDN旨在构建从原始特征到类间边界增强特征的可微映射。所提出的 SDN 是一种高效且灵活的模块，可以轻松插入现有的编码器-解码器分段模型中。大量的实验表明，我们的方法可以在具有挑战性的公共基准上实现对几种典型和最先进的分割基线模型的一致改进。该代码即将发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出语义扩散网络（SDN），通过增强边界特征来改进语义分割模型的预测精度。
AI.motivation: 传统卷积操作会模糊局部细节，导致深度模型难以生成精确的边界预测，需提升边界感知能力。
AI.method: 将边界特征增强建模为各向异性扩散过程，设计可学习的SDN模块，包含参数化语义差分卷积和特征融合模块，构建从原始特征到边界增强特征的可微映射。
AI.result: 在多个公开基准数据集上，SDN显著优于典型分割基线模型，代码即将开源。
AI.conclusion: SDN是一种高效灵活的模块，可无缝集成到现有编码-解码分割框架中，有效提升边界预测性能。

tldr: 本文提出语义扩散网络（SDN），通过增强边界特征来改进语义分割模型的预测精度。
motivation: 传统卷积操作会模糊局部细节，导致深度模型难以生成精确的边界预测，需提升边界感知能力。
method: 将边界特征增强建模为各向异性扩散过程，设计可学习的SDN模块，包含参数化语义差分卷积和特征融合模块，构建从原始特征到边界增强特征的可微映射。
result: 在多个公开基准数据集上，SDN显著优于典型分割基线模型，代码即将开源。
conclusion: SDN是一种高效灵活的模块，可无缝集成到现有编码-解码分割框架中，有效提升边界预测性能。
search_text: Semantic Diffusion Network for Semantic Segmentation 用于语义分割的语义扩散网络 Precise and accurate predictions over boundary areas are essential for semantic segmentation. However, the commonly-used convolutional operators tend to smooth and blur local detail cues, making it difficult for deep models to generate accurate boundary predictions. In this paper, we introduce an operator-level approach to enhance semantic boundary awareness, so as to improve the prediction of the deep semantic segmentation model. Specifically, we first formulate the boundary feature enhancement as an anisotropic diffusion process. We then propose a novel learnable approach called semantic diffusion network (SDN) to approximate the diffusion process, which contains a parameterized semantic difference convolution operator followed by a feature fusion module. Our SDN aims to construct a differentiable mapping from the original feature to the inter-class boundary-enhanced feature. The proposed SDN is an efficient and flexible module that can be easily plugged into existing encoder-decoder segmentation models. Extensive experiments show that our approach can achieve consistent improvements over several typical and state-of-the-art segmentation baseline models on challenging public benchmarks. The code will be released soon. 对边界区域的精确预测对于语义分割至关重要。然而，常用的卷积算子往往会平滑和模糊局部细节线索，使得深度模型难以生成准确的边界预测。在本文中，我们引入了一种算子级方法来增强语义边界感知，从而提高深度语义分割模型的预测。具体来说，我们首先将边界特征增强表示为各向异性扩散过程。然后，我们提出了一种称为语义扩散网络（SDN）的新型可学习方法来近似扩散过程，其中包含参数化语义差异卷积算子，后跟特征融合模块。我们的SDN旨在构建从原始特征到类间边界增强特征的可微映射。所提出的 SDN 是一种高效且灵活的模块，可以轻松插入现有的编码器-解码器分段模型中。大量的实验表明，我们的方法可以在具有挑战性的公共基准上实现对几种典型和最先进的分割基线模型的一致改进。该代码即将发布。 本文提出语义扩散网络（SDN），通过增强边界特征来改进语义分割模型的预测精度。 Haoru Tan,Sitong Wu,Jimin Pi cs.CV

authors_text: Jonathan Hehir,Daniel Ting,Graham Cormode
title: Sketch-Flip-Merge: Mergeable Sketches for Private Distinct Counting
title_zh: 草图翻转合并：用于私有不同计数的可合并草图
abstract: Data sketching is a critical tool for distinct counting, enabling multisets to be represented by compact summaries that admit fast cardinality estimates. Because sketches may be merged to summarize multiset unions, they are a basic building block in data warehouses. Although many practical sketches for cardinality estimation exist, none provide privacy when merging. We propose the first practical cardinality sketches that are simultaneously mergeable, differentially private (DP), and have low empirical errors. These introduce a novel randomized algorithm for performing logical operations on noisy bits, a tight privacy analysis, and provably optimal estimation. Our sketches dramatically outperform existing theoretical solutions in simulations and on real-world data.
summary: Data sketching is a critical tool for distinct counting, enabling multisets to be represented by compact summaries that admit fast cardinality estimates. Because sketches may be merged to summarize multiset unions, they are a basic building block in data warehouses. Although many practical sketches for cardinality estimation exist, none provide privacy when merging. We propose the first practical cardinality sketches that are simultaneously mergeable, differentially private (DP), and have low empirical errors. These introduce a novel randomized algorithm for performing logical operations on noisy bits, a tight privacy analysis, and provably optimal estimation. Our sketches dramatically outperform existing theoretical solutions in simulations and on real-world data.
abstract_zh: 数据草图是进行不同计数的关键工具，使多重集能够通过紧凑的摘要来表示，从而允许快速基数估计。由于草图可以合并以总结多集并集，因此它们是数据仓库中的基本构建块。尽管存在许多用于基数估计的实用草图，但没有一个在合并时提供隐私。我们提出了第一个实用的基数草图，它同时可合并、差分私有（DP）并且具有较低的经验误差。这些引入了一种新颖的随机算法，用于对噪声位执行逻辑运算、严格的隐私分析以及可证明的最佳估计。我们的草图在模拟和现实数据中显着优于现有的理论解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可合并的差分隐私基数估计方法Sketch-Flip-Merge，解决了现有方法在合并时缺乏隐私保护的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基数估计的草图结构在合并操作时无法提供隐私保护，而数据仓库等场景中常需合并多数据集并保障隐私。
AI.method: 设计了一种基于随机化逻辑运算的新型算法，结合紧致的隐私分析和可证明最优的估计方法，实现可合并、差分隐私的草图结构。
AI.result: 在仿真和真实数据集上显著优于现有理论方案，在误差和隐私性之间取得更好平衡。
AI.conclusion: 首次实现了兼具合并性、差分隐私性和低误差的实用基数估计草图，为隐私保护数据分析提供了新工具。

authors_text: Katrina Chen,Mingbin Feng,Tony S. Wirjanto
title: Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection via Dynamic Graph Forecasting
title_zh: 通过动态图预测进行多元时间序列异常检测
abstract: Anomalies in univariate time series often refer to abnormal values and deviations from the temporal patterns from majority of historical observations. In multivariate time series, anomalies also refer to abnormal changes in the inter-series relationship, such as correlation, over time. Existing studies have been able to model such inter-series relationships through graph neural networks. However, most works settle on learning a static graph globally or within a context window to assist a time series forecasting task or a reconstruction task, whose objective is not tailored to explicitly detect the abnormal relationship. Some other works detect anomalies based on reconstructing or forecasting a list of inter-series graphs, which inadvertently weakens their power to capture temporal patterns within the data due to the discrete nature of graphs. In this study, we propose DyGraphAD, a multivariate time series anomaly detection framework based upon a list of dynamic inter-series graphs. The core idea is to detect anomalies based on the deviation of inter-series relationships and intra-series temporal patterns from normal to anomalous states, by leveraging the evolving nature of the graphs in order to assist a graph forecasting task and a time series forecasting task simultaneously. Our numerical experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that DyGraphAD has superior performance than baseline anomaly detection approaches.
summary: Anomalies in univariate time series often refer to abnormal values and deviations from the temporal patterns from majority of historical observations. In multivariate time series, anomalies also refer to abnormal changes in the inter-series relationship, such as correlation, over time. Existing studies have been able to model such inter-series relationships through graph neural networks. However, most works settle on learning a static graph globally or within a context window to assist a time series forecasting task or a reconstruction task, whose objective is not tailored to explicitly detect the abnormal relationship. Some other works detect anomalies based on reconstructing or forecasting a list of inter-series graphs, which inadvertently weakens their power to capture temporal patterns within the data due to the discrete nature of graphs. In this study, we propose DyGraphAD, a multivariate time series anomaly detection framework based upon a list of dynamic inter-series graphs. The core idea is to detect anomalies based on the deviation of inter-series relationships and intra-series temporal patterns from normal to anomalous states, by leveraging the evolving nature of the graphs in order to assist a graph forecasting task and a time series forecasting task simultaneously. Our numerical experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that DyGraphAD has superior performance than baseline anomaly detection approaches.
abstract_zh: 单变量时间序列中的异常通常是指与大多数历史观察的时间模式的异常值和偏差。在多元时间序列中，异常还指序列间关系随时间的异常变化，例如相关性。现有的研究已经能够通过图神经网络对这种系列间关系进行建模。然而，大多数工作都集中在全局或上下文窗口内学习静态图来协助时间序列预测任务或重建任务，其目标并不是为了显式检测异常关系而定制。其他一些工作基于重建或预测一系列间图来检测异常，由于图的离散性质，这无意中削弱了它们捕获数据内时间模式的能力。在本研究中，我们提出了 DyGraphAD，一种基于动态系列间图列表的多元时间序列异常检测框架。其核心思想是根据序列间关系和序列内时间模式从正常状态到异常状态的偏差来检测异常，利用图的演化性质，同时辅助图预测任务和时间序列预测任务。我们对真实世界数据集的数值实验表明，DyGraphAD 比基线异常检测方法具有更优越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于动态图预测的多变量时间序列异常检测框架DyGraphAD，通过动态图捕捉序列间关系变化和时间模式偏差来检测异常。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通过静态图或离散图建模多变量时间序列关系，难以有效捕捉时序模式且未针对性设计异常检测目标，导致检测效果受限。
AI.method: 构建动态图序列建模多变量间关系，通过图预测与时间序列预测联合任务，利用图结构演化特性检测序列间关系及时间模式的异常偏差。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上的实验表明，DyGraphAD相比基线方法在异常检测性能上具有显著优势。
AI.conclusion: 动态图结构能有效表征多变量时间序列的时序依赖与关系变化，为异常检测提供了更精准的建模方式。

In multivariate time series, anomalies also refer to abnormal changes in the inter-series relationship, such as correlation, over time. Existing studies have been able to model such inter-series relationships through graph neural networks. authors_text: Hope Schroeder,Rob Tokanel,Kyle Qian,Khoi Le
title: Location-based AR for Social Justice: Case Studies, Lessons, and Open Challenges
title_zh: 基于位置的 AR 促进社会正义：案例研究、经验教训和公开挑战
abstract: Dear Visitor and Charleston Reconstructed were location-based augmented reality (AR) experiences created between 2018 and 2020 dealing with two controversial monument sites in the US. The projects were motivated by the ability of AR to 1) link layers of context to physical sites in ways that are otherwise difficult or impossible and 2) to visualize changes to physical spaces, potentially inspiring changes to the spaces themselves. We discuss the projects' motivations, designs, and deployments. We reflect on how physical changes to the projects' respective sites radically altered their outcomes, and we describe lessons for future work in location-based AR, particularly for projects in contested spaces.
summary: Dear Visitor and Charleston Reconstructed were location-based augmented reality (AR) experiences created between 2018 and 2020 dealing with two controversial monument sites in the US. The projects were motivated by the ability of AR to 1) link layers of context to physical sites in ways that are otherwise difficult or impossible and 2) to visualize changes to physical spaces, potentially inspiring changes to the spaces themselves. We discuss the projects' motivations, designs, and deployments. We reflect on how physical changes to the projects' respective sites radically altered their outcomes, and we describe lessons for future work in location-based AR, particularly for projects in contested spaces.
abstract_zh: 《亲爱的访客》和《查尔斯顿重建》是 2018 年至 2020 年间创建的基于位置的增强现实 (AR) 体验，涉及美国两个有争议的纪念碑遗址。这些项目的动机是 AR 的能力：1）以其他方式很难或不可能的方式将上下文层链接到物理站点；2）可视化物理空间的变化，可能激发空间本身的变化。我们讨论项目的动机、设计和部署。我们反思了项目各自地点的物理变化如何从根本上改变了其结果，并描述了基于位置的 AR 未来工作的经验教训，特别是对于竞争空间中的项目。

AI.tldr: 本文通过两个案例研究探讨基于位置的增强现实（AR）技术在社会正义议题中的应用，分析其设计、部署及面临的挑战。
AI.motivation: 利用AR技术将历史背景与物理空间结合，并可视化空间变化，以促进对争议性纪念碑遗址的社会讨论与潜在改造。
AI.method: 开发两个基于位置的AR项目（Dear Visitor与Charleston Reconstructed），通过实地部署观察用户互动，并分析物理环境变化对项目效果的影响。
AI.result: 物理空间的改变显著影响AR体验效果，揭示了在争议性场所进行AR项目时需动态调整设计，并重视社区参与与环境因素。
AI.conclusion: 未来基于位置的AR社会项目需平衡技术设计与现实环境变化，同时加强跨学科合作以应对复杂的社会议题。

search_text: Location-based AR for Social Justice: Case Studies, Lessons, and Open Challenges 基于位置的 AR 促进社会正义：案例研究、经验教训和公开挑战 Dear Visitor and Charleston Reconstructed were location-based augmented reality (AR) experiences created between 2018 and 2020 dealing with two controversial monument sites in the US. The projects were motivated by the ability of AR to 1) link layers of context to physical sites in ways that are otherwise difficult or impossible and 2) to visualize changes to physical spaces, potentially inspiring changes to the spaces themselves. We discuss the projects' motivations, designs, and deployments. We reflect on how physical changes to the projects' respective sites radically altered their outcomes, and we describe lessons for future work in location-based AR, particularly for projects in contested spaces. 《亲爱的访客》和《查尔斯顿重建》是 2018 年至 2020 年间创建的基于位置的增强现实 (AR) 体验，涉及美国两个有争议的纪念碑遗址。这些项目的动机是 AR 的能力：1）以其他方式很难或不可能的方式将上下文层链接到物理站点；2）可视化物理空间的变化，可能激发空间本身的变化。我们讨论项目的动机、设计和部署。我们反思了项目各自地点的物理变化如何从根本上改变了其结果，并描述了基于位置的 AR 未来工作的经验教训，特别是对于竞争空间中的项目。 本文通过两个案例研究探讨基于位置的增强现实（AR）技术在社会正义议题中的应用，分析其设计、部署及面临的挑战。 Hope Schroeder,Rob Tokanel,Kyle Qian,Khoi Le cs.HC

authors_text: Kausik Lakkaraju,Biplav Srivastava,Marco Valtorta
title: Rating Sentiment Analysis Systems for Bias through a Causal Lens
title_zh: 通过因果视角评估情绪分析系统的偏差
abstract: Sentiment Analysis Systems (SASs) are data-driven Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems that, given a piece of text, assign one or more numbers conveying the polarity and emotional intensity expressed in the input. Like other automatic machine learning systems, they have also been known to exhibit model uncertainty where a (small) change in the input leads to drastic swings in the output. This can be especially problematic when inputs are related to protected features like gender or race since such behavior can be perceived as a lack of fairness, i.e., bias. We introduce a novel method to assess and rate SASs where inputs are perturbed in a controlled causal setting to test if the output sentiment is sensitive to protected variables even when other components of the textual input, e.g., chosen emotion words, are fixed. We then use the result to assign labels (ratings) at fine-grained and overall levels to convey the robustness of the SAS to input changes. The ratings serve as a principled basis to compare SASs and choose among them based on behavior. It benefits all users, especially developers who reuse off-the-shelf SASs to build larger AI systems but do not have access to their code or training data to compare.
summary: Sentiment Analysis Systems (SASs) are data-driven Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems that, given a piece of text, assign one or more numbers conveying the polarity and emotional intensity expressed in the input. Like other automatic machine learning systems, they have also been known to exhibit model uncertainty where a (small) change in the input leads to drastic swings in the output. This can be especially problematic when inputs are related to protected features like gender or race since such behavior can be perceived as a lack of fairness, i.e., bias. We introduce a novel method to assess and rate SASs where inputs are perturbed in a controlled causal setting to test if the output sentiment is sensitive to protected variables even when other components of the textual input, e.g., chosen emotion words, are fixed. We then use the result to assign labels (ratings) at fine-grained and overall levels to convey the robustness of the SAS to input changes. The ratings serve as a principled basis to compare SASs and choose among them based on behavior. It benefits all users, especially developers who reuse off-the-shelf SASs to build larger AI systems but do not have access to their code or training data to compare.
abstract_zh: 情感分析系统 (SAS) 是数据驱动的人工智能 (AI) 系统，根据给定的一段文本，分配一个或多个数字来传达输入中表达的极性和情感强度。与其他自动机器学习系统一样，它们也表现出模型的不确定性，其中输入的（微小）变化会导致输出的剧烈波动。当输入与性别或种族等受保护特征相关时，这可能尤其成问题，因为这种行为可能被视为缺乏公平，即偏见。我们引入了一种新的方法来评估和评级 SAS，其中输入在受控因果设置中受到扰动，以测试输出情绪是否对受保护的变量敏感，即使文本输入的其他组成部分（例如，选择的情感词）是固定的。然后，我们使用结果在细粒度和整体级别上分配标签（评级），以传达 SAS 对输入变化的稳健性。这些评级是比较 SAS 并根据行为进行选择的原则基础。它使所有用户受益，特别是那些重复使用现成的 SAS 来构建更大的 AI 系统但无法访问其代码或训练数据进行比较的开发人员。

AI.tldr: 提出一种通过因果干预评估情感分析系统（SASs）对受保护变量敏感性的方法，并为其分配鲁棒性评级。
AI.motivation: 现有SASs在受保护特征（如性别、种族）上可能存在偏差，且缺乏系统化方法评估其鲁棒性，影响开发者选择和使用。
AI.method: 通过因果干预，在控制其他文本成分的情况下扰动输入，测试输出情感对受保护变量的敏感性，并基于结果分配细粒度和总体评级。
AI.result: 通过该方法可生成细粒度和总体评级，用于系统比较和选择，尤其帮助无法访问源代码的开发者。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为评估SASs的鲁棒性和公平性提供了一种系统化方案，有助于开发者选择更可靠的情感分析系统，推动公平AI发展。

authors_text: Jacob Brown,Xi Jiang,Van Tran,Arjun Nitin Bhagoji,Nguyen Phong Hoang,Nick Feamster,Prateek Mittal,Vinod Yegneswaran
title: Augmenting Rule-based DNS Censorship Detection at Scale with Machine Learning
title_zh: 通过机器学习大规模增强基于规则的 DNS 审查检测
comment: To appear in Proceedings of the 29th ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining (KDD '23)
comments: To appear in Proceedings of the 29th ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining (KDD '23)
abstract: The proliferation of global censorship has led to the development of a plethora of measurement platforms to monitor and expose it. Censorship of the domain name system (DNS) is a key mechanism used across different countries. It is currently detected by applying heuristics to samples of DNS queries and responses (probes) for specific destinations. These heuristics, however, are both platform-specific and have been found to be brittle when censors change their blocking behavior, necessitating a more reliable automated process for detecting censorship. In this paper, we explore how machine learning (ML) models can (1) help streamline the detection process, (2) improve the potential of using large-scale datasets for censorship detection, and (3) discover new censorship instances and blocking signatures missed by existing heuristic methods. Our study shows that supervised models, trained using expert-derived labels on instances of known anomalies and possible censorship, can learn the detection heuristics employed by different measurement platforms. More crucially, we find that unsupervised models, trained solely on uncensored instances, can identify new instances and variations of censorship missed by existing heuristics. Moreover, both methods demonstrate the capability to uncover a substantial number of new DNS blocking signatures, i.e., injected fake IP addresses overlooked by existing heuristics. These results are underpinned by an important methodological finding: comparing the outputs of models trained using the same probes but with labels arising from independent processes allows us to more reliably detect cases of censorship in the absence of ground-truth labels of censorship.
summary: The proliferation of global censorship has led to the development of a plethora of measurement platforms to monitor and expose it. Censorship of the domain name system (DNS) is a key mechanism used across different countries. It is currently detected by applying heuristics to samples of DNS queries and responses (probes) for specific destinations. These heuristics, however, are both platform-specific and have been found to be brittle when censors change their blocking behavior, necessitating a more reliable automated process for detecting censorship. In this paper, we explore how machine learning (ML) models can (1) help streamline the detection process, (2) improve the potential of using large-scale datasets for censorship detection, and (3) discover new censorship instances and blocking signatures missed by existing heuristic methods. Our study shows that supervised models, trained using expert-derived labels on instances of known anomalies and possible censorship, can learn the detection heuristics employed by different measurement platforms. More crucially, we find that unsupervised models, trained solely on uncensored instances, can identify new instances and variations of censorship missed by existing heuristics. Moreover, both methods demonstrate the capability to uncover a substantial number of new DNS blocking signatures, i.e., injected fake IP addresses overlooked by existing heuristics. These results are underpinned by an important methodological finding: comparing the outputs of models trained using the same probes but with labels arising from independent processes allows us to more reliably detect cases of censorship in the absence of ground-truth labels of censorship.
abstract_zh: 全球审查制度的扩散导致了过多的衡量平台的发展来监控和揭露它。域名系统 (DNS) 审查是不同国家/地区使用的关键机制。目前，它是通过对特定目的地的 DNS 查询和响应（探测）样本应用启发式方法来检测的。然而，这些启发式方法都是特定于平台的，并且当审查机构改变其阻止行为时，它们很脆弱，因此需要更可靠的自动化流程来检测审查制度。在本文中，我们探讨了机器学习（ML）模型如何（1）帮助简化检测过程，（2）提高使用大规模数据集进行审查检测的潜力，以及（3）发现新的审查实例并阻止现有启发式方法遗漏的签名。我们的研究表明，使用专家衍生的标签对已知异常和可能的审查实例进行训练的监督模型可以学习不同测量平台采用的检测启发式方法。更重要的是，我们发现仅在未经审查的实例上进行训练的无监督模型可以识别现有启发法遗漏的新实例和审查制度的变化。此外，这两种方法都展示了发现大量新的 DNS 阻止签名的能力，即现有启发式方法忽略的注入的虚假 IP 地址。这些结果得到了一个重要的方法论发现的支持：比较使用相同探针但具有独立过程产生的标签训练的模型的输出，使我们能够在缺乏审查制度的真实标签的情况下更可靠地检测审查案例。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用机器学习增强基于规则的DNS审查检测方法，以应对传统启发式方法的局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有DNS审查检测依赖平台特定的启发式规则，当审查策略变化时易失效，需更可靠的自动化检测方法。
AI.method: 结合监督学习（基于专家标注的异常样本训练）与无监督学习（仅使用未审查数据训练），通过对比不同标签来源模型的输出结果提升检测可靠性。
AI.result: 模型成功发现传统方法遗漏的新审查实例和伪造IP地址签名，且在大规模数据集上验证了有效性。
AI.conclusion: 机器学习显著提升DNS审查检测能力，尤其在无真实标签场景下，跨模型对比方法可有效识别新型审查模式。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出利用机器学习增强基于规则的DNS审查检测方法，以应对传统启发式方法的局限性。

conclusion: 机器学习显著提升DNS审查检测能力，尤其在无真实标签场景下，跨模型对比方法可有效识别新型审查模式。
search_text: Augmenting Rule-based DNS Censorship Detection at Scale with Machine Learning 通过机器学习大规模增强基于规则的 DNS 审查检测 The proliferation of global censorship has led to the development of a plethora of measurement platforms to monitor and expose it. Censorship of the domain name system (DNS) is a key mechanism used across different countries. It is currently detected by applying heuristics to samples of DNS queries and responses (probes) for specific destinations. These heuristics, however, are both platform-specific and have been found to be brittle when censors change their blocking behavior, necessitating a more reliable automated process for detecting censorship. In this paper, we explore how machine learning (ML) models can (1) help streamline the detection process, (2) improve the potential of using large-scale datasets for censorship detection, and (3) discover new censorship instances and blocking signatures missed by existing heuristic methods. Our study shows that supervised models, trained using expert-derived labels on instances of known anomalies and possible censorship, can learn the detection heuristics employed by different measurement platforms. More crucially, we find that unsupervised models, trained solely on uncensored instances, can identify new instances and variations of censorship missed by existing heuristics. Moreover, both methods demonstrate the capability to uncover a substantial number of new DNS blocking signatures, i.e., injected fake IP addresses overlooked by existing heuristics. These results are underpinned by an important methodological finding: comparing the outputs of models trained using the same probes but with labels arising from independent processes allows us to more reliably detect cases of censorship in the absence of ground-truth labels of censorship. 全球审查制度的扩散导致了过多的衡量平台的发展来监控和揭露它。域名系统 (DNS) 审查是不同国家/地区使用的关键机制。目前，它是通过对特定目的地的 DNS 查询和响应（探测）样本应用启发式方法来检测的。然而，这些启发式方法都是特定于平台的，并且当审查机构改变其阻止行为时，它们很脆弱，因此需要更可靠的自动化流程来检测审查制度。在本文中，我们探讨了机器学习（ML）模型如何（1）帮助简化检测过程，（2）提高使用大规模数据集进行审查检测的潜力，以及（3）发现新的审查实例并阻止现有启发式方法遗漏的签名。我们的研究表明，使用专家衍生的标签对已知异常和可能的审查实例进行训练的监督模型可以学习不同测量平台采用的检测启发式方法。更重要的是，我们发现仅在未经审查的实例上进行训练的无监督模型可以识别现有启发法遗漏的新实例和审查制度的变化。此外，这两种方法都展示了发现大量新的 DNS 阻止签名的能力，即现有启发式方法忽略的注入的虚假 IP 地址。这些结果得到了一个重要的方法论发现的支持：比较使用相同探针但具有独立过程产生的标签训练的模型的输出，使我们能够在缺乏审查制度的真实标签的情况下更可靠地检测审查案例。 本文提出利用机器学习增强基于规则的DNS审查检测方法，以应对传统启发式方法的局限性。 Jacob Brown,Xi Jiang,Van Tran,Arjun Nitin Bhagoji,Nguyen Phong Hoang,Nick Feamster,Prateek Mittal,Vinod Yegneswaran cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CY,cs.NI

authors_text: Fausto Navarro,Daniel Hall,Tamas Budavari,Yashil Sukurdeep
title: Learning the Night Sky with Deep Generative Priors
title_zh: 用深度生成先验学习夜空
abstract: Recovering sharper images from blurred observations, referred to as deconvolution, is an ill-posed problem where classical approaches often produce unsatisfactory results. In ground-based astronomy, combining multiple exposures to achieve images with higher signal-to-noise ratios is complicated by the variation of point-spread functions across exposures due to atmospheric effects. We develop an unsupervised multi-frame method for denoising, deblurring, and coadding images inspired by deep generative priors. We use a carefully chosen convolutional neural network architecture that combines information from multiple observations, regularizes the joint likelihood over these observations, and allows us to impose desired constraints, such as non-negativity of pixel values in the sharp, restored image. With an eye towards the Rubin Observatory, we analyze 4K by 4K Hyper Suprime-Cam exposures and obtain preliminary results which yield promising restored images and extracted source lists.
summary: Recovering sharper images from blurred observations, referred to as deconvolution, is an ill-posed problem where classical approaches often produce unsatisfactory results. In ground-based astronomy, combining multiple exposures to achieve images with higher signal-to-noise ratios is complicated by the variation of point-spread functions across exposures due to atmospheric effects. We develop an unsupervised multi-frame method for denoising, deblurring, and coadding images inspired by deep generative priors. We use a carefully chosen convolutional neural network architecture that combines information from multiple observations, regularizes the joint likelihood over these observations, and allows us to impose desired constraints, such as non-negativity of pixel values in the sharp, restored image. With an eye towards the Rubin Observatory, we analyze 4K by 4K Hyper Suprime-Cam exposures and obtain preliminary results which yield promising restored images and extracted source lists.
abstract_zh: 从模糊的观察中恢复更清晰的图像（称为反卷积）是一个不适定的问题，经典方法常常产生不令人满意的结果。在地面天文学中，由于大气影响，曝光间的点扩散函数会发生变化，因此结合多次曝光来获得具有更高信噪比的图像变得复杂。受深层生成先验的启发，我们开发了一种无监督多帧方法，用于去噪、去模糊和编码图像。我们使用精心选择的卷积神经网络架构，该架构结合了来自多个观察的信息，对这些观察的联合似然进行正则化，并允许我们施加所需的约束，例如清晰的恢复图像中像素值的非负性。我们着眼于鲁宾天文台，分析了 4K 到 4K Hyper Suprime-Cam 的曝光，并获得了初步结果，这些结果产生了有希望的恢复图像和提取的源列表。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度生成先验的无监督多帧方法，用于天文图像的去噪、去模糊和叠加处理。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理大气扰动导致的点扩散函数变化时效果不佳，需更有效的多帧图像恢复技术以提升天文观测质量。
AI.method: 采用卷积神经网络架构结合多帧观测数据，通过正则化联合似然函数并施加像素非负性约束进行图像恢复。
AI.result: 在4K×4K Hyper Suprime-Cam数据上实现初步成功，获得高质量恢复图像及源列表，验证方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为天文图像处理提供了新思路，尤其适用于 Rubin 望远镜等大规模观测项目，具有重要应用前景。

authors_text: Lingfeng Shen,Ze Zhang,Haiyun Jiang,Ying Chen
title: TextShield: Beyond Successfully Detecting Adversarial Sentences in Text Classification
title_zh: TextShield：超越在文本分类中成功检测对抗性句子
abstract: Adversarial attack serves as a major challenge for neural network models in NLP, which precludes the model's deployment in safety-critical applications. A recent line of work, detection-based defense, aims to distinguish adversarial sentences from benign ones. However, {the core limitation of previous detection methods is being incapable of giving correct predictions on adversarial sentences unlike defense methods from other paradigms.} To solve this issue, this paper proposes TextShield: (1) we discover a link between text attack and saliency information, and then we propose a saliency-based detector, which can effectively detect whether an input sentence is adversarial or not. (2) We design a saliency-based corrector, which converts the detected adversary sentences to benign ones. By combining the saliency-based detector and corrector, TextShield extends the detection-only paradigm to a detection-correction paradigm, thus filling the gap in the existing detection-based defense. Comprehensive experiments show that (a) TextShield consistently achieves higher or comparable performance than state-of-the-art defense methods across various attacks on different benchmarks. (b) our saliency-based detector outperforms existing detectors for detecting adversarial sentences.
summary: Adversarial attack serves as a major challenge for neural network models in NLP, which precludes the model's deployment in safety-critical applications. A recent line of work, detection-based defense, aims to distinguish adversarial sentences from benign ones. However, {the core limitation of previous detection methods is being incapable of giving correct predictions on adversarial sentences unlike defense methods from other paradigms.} To solve this issue, this paper proposes TextShield: (1) we discover a link between text attack and saliency information, and then we propose a saliency-based detector, which can effectively detect whether an input sentence is adversarial or not. (2) We design a saliency-based corrector, which converts the detected adversary sentences to benign ones. By combining the saliency-based detector and corrector, TextShield extends the detection-only paradigm to a detection-correction paradigm, thus filling the gap in the existing detection-based defense. Comprehensive experiments show that (a) TextShield consistently achieves higher or comparable performance than state-of-the-art defense methods across various attacks on different benchmarks. (b) our saliency-based detector outperforms existing detectors for detecting adversarial sentences.
abstract_zh: 对抗性攻击是 NLP 中神经网络模型的主要挑战，这阻碍了模型在安全关键型应用中的部署。最近的一项工作是基于检测的防御，旨在区分对抗性句子和良性句子。然而，{以前的检测方法的核心局限性是无法像其他范式的防御方法那样对对抗性句子给出正确的预测。}为了解决这个问题，本文提出了 TextShield：（1）我们发现文本攻击和显着性信息之间的联系，然后我们提出了一种基于显着性的检测器，它可以有效地检测输入句子是否是对抗性的。 （2）我们设计了一个基于显着性的校正器，它将检测到的对手句子转换为良性句子。通过结合基于显着性的检测器和校正器，TextShield将仅检测范式扩展到检测校正范式，从而填补了现有基于检测的防御的空白。综合实验表明，(a) TextShield 在不同基准的各种攻击中始终能够实现比最先进的防御方法更高或相当的性能。 (b) 我们的基于显着性的检测器在检测对抗性句子方面优于现有的检测器。

AI.tldr: 提出TextShield方法，通过显著性信息检测并校正对抗样本，提升文本分类模型的鲁棒性
AI.motivation: 现有检测方法无法正确识别对抗样本，导致防御效果有限，需更有效的检测与校正方法
AI.method: 设计基于显著性信息的检测器识别对抗样本，并结合校正器将其转换为良性样本，扩展检测范式为检测-校正范式
AI.result: TextShield在多个基准测试中优于现有方法，显著性检测器性能更优
AI.conclusion: TextShield通过检测-校正范式有效提升防御效果，填补了检测防御的空白

authors_text: Arshdeep Sekhon,Hanjie Chen,Aman Shrivastava,Zhe Wang,Yangfeng Ji,Yanjun Qi
title: Improving Interpretability via Explicit Word Interaction Graph Layer
title_zh: 通过显式词交互图形层提高可解释性
abstract: Recent NLP literature has seen growing interest in improving model interpretability. Along this direction, we propose a trainable neural network layer that learns a global interaction graph between words and then selects more informative words using the learned word interactions. Our layer, we call WIGRAPH, can plug into any neural network-based NLP text classifiers right after its word embedding layer. Across multiple SOTA NLP models and various NLP datasets, we demonstrate that adding the WIGRAPH layer substantially improves NLP models' interpretability and enhances models' prediction performance at the same time.
summary: Recent NLP literature has seen growing interest in improving model interpretability. Along this direction, we propose a trainable neural network layer that learns a global interaction graph between words and then selects more informative words using the learned word interactions. Our layer, we call WIGRAPH, can plug into any neural network-based NLP text classifiers right after its word embedding layer. Across multiple SOTA NLP models and various NLP datasets, we demonstrate that adding the WIGRAPH layer substantially improves NLP models' interpretability and enhances models' prediction performance at the same time.
abstract_zh: 最近的 NLP 文献对提高模型可解释性越来越感兴趣。沿着这个方向，我们提出了一个可训练的神经网络层，它学习单词之间的全局交互图，然后使用学习到的单词交互选择信息更丰富的单词。我们的层，我们称之为 WIGRAPH，可以在其词嵌入层之后插入任何基于神经网络的 NLP 文本分类器。在多个 SOTA NLP 模型和各种 NLP 数据集上，我们证明添加 WIGRAPH 层可以显着提高 NLP 模型的可解释性，同时增强模型的预测性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为WIGRAPH的可训练神经网络层，通过学习词间交互图提升NLP模型的可解释性并增强预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有NLP模型普遍缺乏可解释性，且改进可解释性的方法常以牺牲性能为代价，需同时提升两者。
AI.method: 设计WIGRAPH层在词嵌入后构建全局词交互图，通过图结构学习选择信息量更大的关键词。
AI.result: 在多个SOTA模型和数据集上验证，WIGRAPH显著提升模型可解释性同时改善分类准确率。
AI.conclusion: WIGRAPH通过显式建模词交互有效平衡可解释性与性能，为可解释NLP提供新方法论。

search_text: Improving Interpretability via Explicit Word Interaction Graph Layer 通过显式词交互图形层提高可解释性 Recent NLP literature has seen growing interest in improving model interpretability. Along this direction, we propose a trainable neural network layer that learns a global interaction graph between words and then selects more informative words using the learned word interactions. Our layer, we call WIGRAPH, can plug into any neural network-based NLP text classifiers right after its word embedding layer. Across multiple SOTA NLP models and various NLP datasets, we demonstrate that adding the WIGRAPH layer substantially improves NLP models' interpretability and enhances models' prediction performance at the same time. 最近的 NLP 文献对提高模型可解释性越来越感兴趣。沿着这个方向，我们提出了一个可训练的神经网络层，它学习单词之间的全局交互图，然后使用学习到的单词交互选择信息更丰富的单词。我们的层，我们称之为 WIGRAPH，可以在其词嵌入层之后插入任何基于神经网络的 NLP 文本分类器。在多个 SOTA NLP 模型和各种 NLP 数据集上，我们证明添加 WIGRAPH 层可以显着提高 NLP 模型的可解释性，同时增强模型的预测性能。 本文提出一种名为WIGRAPH的可训练神经网络层，通过学习词间交互图提升NLP模型的可解释性并增强预测性能。 Arshdeep Sekhon,Hanjie Chen,Aman Shrivastava,Zhe Wang,Yangfeng Ji,Yanjun Qi cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Homanga Bharadhwaj,Abhinav Gupta,Shubham Tulsiani,Vikash Kumar
title: Zero-Shot Robot Manipulation from Passive Human Videos
title_zh: 从被动人类视频中进行零射击机器人操作
abstract: Can we learn robot manipulation for everyday tasks, only by watching videos of humans doing arbitrary tasks in different unstructured settings? Unlike widely adopted strategies of learning task-specific behaviors or direct imitation of a human video, we develop a a framework for extracting agent-agnostic action representations from human videos, and then map it to the agent's embodiment during deployment. Our framework is based on predicting plausible human hand trajectories given an initial image of a scene. After training this prediction model on a diverse set of human videos from the internet, we deploy the trained model zero-shot for physical robot manipulation tasks, after appropriate transformations to the robot's embodiment. This simple strategy lets us solve coarse manipulation tasks like opening and closing drawers, pushing, and tool use, without access to any in-domain robot manipulation trajectories. Our real-world deployment results establish a strong baseline for action prediction information that can be acquired from diverse arbitrary videos of human activities, and be useful for zero-shot robotic manipulation in unseen scenes.
summary: Can we learn robot manipulation for everyday tasks, only by watching videos of humans doing arbitrary tasks in different unstructured settings? Unlike widely adopted strategies of learning task-specific behaviors or direct imitation of a human video, we develop a a framework for extracting agent-agnostic action representations from human videos, and then map it to the agent's embodiment during deployment. Our framework is based on predicting plausible human hand trajectories given an initial image of a scene. After training this prediction model on a diverse set of human videos from the internet, we deploy the trained model zero-shot for physical robot manipulation tasks, after appropriate transformations to the robot's embodiment. This simple strategy lets us solve coarse manipulation tasks like opening and closing drawers, pushing, and tool use, without access to any in-domain robot manipulation trajectories. Our real-world deployment results establish a strong baseline for action prediction information that can be acquired from diverse arbitrary videos of human activities, and be useful for zero-shot robotic manipulation in unseen scenes.
abstract_zh: 我们能否仅通过观看人类在不同的非结构化环境中执行任意任务的视频来学习机器人的日常任务操作？与广泛采用的学习特定任务行为或直接模仿人类视频的策略不同，我们开发了一个框架，用于从人类视频中提取与代理无关的动作表示，然后在部署期间将其映射到代理的体现。我们的框架基于给定场景的初始图像来预测合理的人手轨迹。在对来自互联网的不同人类视频集训练该预测模型后，在对机器人的实施例进行适当转换后，我们将训练好的模型零样本部署用于物理机器人操作任务。这种简单的策略让我们能够解决粗略的操作任务，例如打开和关闭抽屉、推动和工具使用，而无需访问任何域内机器人操作轨迹。我们的现实世界部署结果为动作预测信息建立了强大的基线，这些信息可以从人类活动的各种任意视频中获取，并且对于未见过的场景中的零镜头机器人操作很有用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种从被动观察人类视频中学习零样本机器人操作的方法，无需直接模仿或特定任务的数据。
AI.motivation: 传统机器人学习依赖特定任务数据或直接模仿，难以处理未见过的场景和任务。本文旨在通过人类视频学习通用操作表示，实现零样本机器人操作。
AI.method: 通过预测场景初始图像中的人手轨迹，训练模型提取与代理无关的动作表示，并将其映射到机器人执行器，实现跨模态动作迁移。
AI.result: 在现实场景中成功完成抽屉开关、推动和工具使用等粗粒度操作任务，无需任何机器人操作轨迹数据。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为从任意人类活动视频中获取动作预测信息提供了强基线，证明了零样本机器人操作在未见过场景中的可行性。

authors_text: Joe Toplyn
title: Witscript: A System for Generating Improvised Jokes in a Conversation
title_zh: Witscript：在对话中生成即兴笑话的系统
comment: 10 pages. Published in the Proceedings of the 12th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC 2021), pages 22-31
comments: 10 pages. Published in the Proceedings of the 12th International Conference on Computational Creativity (ICCC 2021), pages 22-31
abstract: A chatbot is perceived as more humanlike and likeable if it includes some jokes in its output. But most existing joke generators were not designed to be integrated into chatbots. This paper presents Witscript, a novel joke generation system that can improvise original, contextually relevant jokes, such as humorous responses during a conversation. The system is based on joke writing algorithms created by an expert comedy writer. Witscript employs well-known tools of natural language processing to extract keywords from a topic sentence and, using wordplay, to link those keywords and related words to create a punch line. Then a pretrained neural network language model that has been fine-tuned on a dataset of TV show monologue jokes is used to complete the joke response by filling the gap between the topic sentence and the punch line. A method of internal scoring filters out jokes that don't meet a preset standard of quality. Human evaluators judged Witscript's responses to input sentences to be jokes more than 40% of the time. This is evidence that Witscript represents an important next step toward giving a chatbot a humanlike sense of humor.
summary: A chatbot is perceived as more humanlike and likeable if it includes some jokes in its output. But most existing joke generators were not designed to be integrated into chatbots. This paper presents Witscript, a novel joke generation system that can improvise original, contextually relevant jokes, such as humorous responses during a conversation. The system is based on joke writing algorithms created by an expert comedy writer. Witscript employs well-known tools of natural language processing to extract keywords from a topic sentence and, using wordplay, to link those keywords and related words to create a punch line. Then a pretrained neural network language model that has been fine-tuned on a dataset of TV show monologue jokes is used to complete the joke response by filling the gap between the topic sentence and the punch line. A method of internal scoring filters out jokes that don't meet a preset standard of quality. Human evaluators judged Witscript's responses to input sentences to be jokes more than 40% of the time. This is evidence that Witscript represents an important next step toward giving a chatbot a humanlike sense of humor.
abstract_zh: 如果聊天机器人的输出中包含一些笑话，就会被认为更人性化、更讨人喜欢。但大多数现有的笑话生成器并不是为了集成到聊天机器人中而设计的。本文介绍了 Witscript，这是一种新颖的笑话生成系统，可以即兴创作原创的、与上下文相关的笑话，例如对话中的幽默反应。该系统基于喜剧专家创建的笑话编写算法。 Witscript 采用众所周知的自然语言处理工具从主题句中提取关键字，并使用双关语将这些关键字和相关单词链接起来以创建妙语。然后，使用在电视节目独白笑话数据集上进行微调的预训练神经网络语言模型，通过填补主题句和妙语之间的空白来完成笑话响应。内部评分方法可以过滤掉不符合预设质量标准的笑话。人类评估者在超过 40% 的情况下判断 Witscript 对输入句子的反应是笑话。这证明 Witscript 代表着向聊天机器人赋予人类般的幽默感迈出了重要的一步。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Witscript系统，旨在为聊天机器人生成上下文相关的即兴笑话，提升其拟人化和亲和力。
AI.motivation: 现有笑话生成器难以集成到聊天机器人中，而加入幽默元素可显著提升聊天机器人的拟人化效果和用户好感度。
AI.method: 结合喜剧作家的算法与自然语言处理技术，通过提取关键词、双关语关联及预训练神经网络模型生成笑话，并利用内部评分过滤低质量内容。
AI.result: 人类评估显示，Witscript生成的响应中有超过40%被判定为有效笑话，证明其生成能力达到一定质量标准。
AI.conclusion: Witscript为聊天机器人赋予类人幽默感提供了重要进展，是对话系统拟人化的关键一步。

authors_text: Santiago Balseiro,Rachitesh Kumar,Vahab Mirrokni,Balasubramanian Sivan,Di Wang
title: Robust Budget Pacing with a Single Sample
title_zh: 单一样本的稳健预算节奏
abstract: Major Internet advertising platforms offer budget pacing tools as a standard service for advertisers to manage their ad campaigns. Given the inherent non-stationarity in an advertiser's value and also competing advertisers' values over time, a commonly used approach is to learn a target expenditure plan that specifies a target spend as a function of time, and then run a controller that tracks this plan. This raises the question: how many historical samples are required to learn a good expenditure plan? We study this question by considering an advertiser repeatedly participating in $T$ second-price auctions, where the tuple of her value and the highest competing bid is drawn from an unknown time-varying distribution. The advertiser seeks to maximize her total utility subject to her budget constraint. Prior work has shown the sufficiency of $T\log T$ samples per distribution to achieve the optimal $O(\sqrt{T})$-regret. We dramatically improve this state-of-the-art and show that just one sample per distribution is enough to achieve the near-optimal $\tilde O(\sqrt{T})$-regret, while still being robust to noise in the sampling distributions.
summary: Major Internet advertising platforms offer budget pacing tools as a standard service for advertisers to manage their ad campaigns. Given the inherent non-stationarity in an advertiser's value and also competing advertisers' values over time, a commonly used approach is to learn a target expenditure plan that specifies a target spend as a function of time, and then run a controller that tracks this plan. This raises the question: how many historical samples are required to learn a good expenditure plan? We study this question by considering an advertiser repeatedly participating in $T$ second-price auctions, where the tuple of her value and the highest competing bid is drawn from an unknown time-varying distribution. The advertiser seeks to maximize her total utility subject to her budget constraint. Prior work has shown the sufficiency of $T\log T$ samples per distribution to achieve the optimal $O(\sqrt{T})$-regret. We dramatically improve this state-of-the-art and show that just one sample per distribution is enough to achieve the near-optimal $\tilde O(\sqrt{T})$-regret, while still being robust to noise in the sampling distributions.
abstract_zh: 主要互联网广告平台提供预算节奏工具作为广告商管理广告活动的标准服务。考虑到广告商价值以及竞争广告商价值随着时间的推移而固有的非平稳性，一种常用的方法是学习一个目标支出计划，该计划将目标支出指定为时间的函数，然后运行一个跟踪该计划的控制器。这就提出了一个问题：需要多少历史样本才能学习出一个好的支出计划？我们通过考虑重复参与 $T$ 第二价格拍卖的广告商来研究这个问题，其中她的价值和最高竞争出价的元组是从未知的时变分布中得出的。广告商寻求在预算限制下最大化其总效用。先前的工作已经表明每个分布的 $T\log T$ 样本足以实现最佳 $O(\sqrt{T})$-regret。我们极大地改进了这一最先进的技术，并表明每个分布只需一个样本就足以实现接近最优的 $\tilde O(\sqrt{T})$-regret，同时仍然对采样分布中的噪声具有鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在时间变化的广告拍卖环境中，如何通过极少的历史样本学习有效的预算分配策略以最小化遗憾。
AI.motivation: 广告平台需要处理动态变化的广告主价值和竞争者出价，传统方法需大量样本，而实际场景中样本可能有限，需更高效算法。
AI.method: 提出基于单一样本估计分布的鲁棒控制方法，结合在线学习与预算约束优化，设计对噪声分布具有鲁棒性的支出计划跟踪器。
AI.result: 证明仅需每个分布一个样本即可达到近似最优的O(√T)遗憾，相较先前T log T样本量有显著改进，并保持对噪声的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在样本效率上实现突破，为实际广告预算管理提供理论支持，尤其适用于样本稀缺的动态竞价环境。

authors_text: A. Ćiprijanović,A. Lewis,K. Pedro,S. Madireddy,B. Nord,G. N. Perdue,S. M. Wild
title: DeepAstroUDA: Semi-Supervised Universal Domain Adaptation for Cross-Survey Galaxy Morphology Classification and Anomaly Detection
title_zh: DeepAstroUDA：用于交叉调查星系形态分类和异常检测的半监督通用域适应
comment: Accepted in Machine Learning Science and Technology (MLST); 24 pages, 14 figures
comments: Accepted in Machine Learning Science and Technology (MLST); 24 pages, 14 figures
abstract: Artificial intelligence methods show great promise in increasing the quality and speed of work with large astronomical datasets, but the high complexity of these methods leads to the extraction of dataset-specific, non-robust features. Therefore, such methods do not generalize well across multiple datasets. We present a universal domain adaptation method, \textit{DeepAstroUDA}, as an approach to overcome this challenge. This algorithm performs semi-supervised domain adaptation and can be applied to datasets with different data distributions and class overlaps. Non-overlapping classes can be present in any of the two datasets (the labeled source domain, or the unlabeled target domain), and the method can even be used in the presence of unknown classes. We apply our method to three examples of galaxy morphology classification tasks of different complexities ($3$-class and $10$-class problems), with anomaly detection: 1) datasets created after different numbers of observing years from a single survey (LSST mock data of $1$ and $10$ years of observations); 2) data from different surveys (SDSS and DECaLS); and 3) data from observing fields with different depths within one survey (wide field and Stripe 82 deep field of SDSS). For the first time, we demonstrate the successful use of domain adaptation between very discrepant observational datasets. \textit{DeepAstroUDA} is capable of bridging the gap between two astronomical surveys, increasing classification accuracy in both domains (up to $40\%$ on the unlabeled data), and making model performance consistent across datasets. Furthermore, our method also performs well as an anomaly detection algorithm and successfully clusters unknown class samples even in the unlabeled target dataset.
summary: Artificial intelligence methods show great promise in increasing the quality and speed of work with large astronomical datasets, but the high complexity of these methods leads to the extraction of dataset-specific, non-robust features. Therefore, such methods do not generalize well across multiple datasets. We present a universal domain adaptation method, \textit{DeepAstroUDA}, as an approach to overcome this challenge. This algorithm performs semi-supervised domain adaptation and can be applied to datasets with different data distributions and class overlaps. Non-overlapping classes can be present in any of the two datasets (the labeled source domain, or the unlabeled target domain), and the method can even be used in the presence of unknown classes. We apply our method to three examples of galaxy morphology classification tasks of different complexities ($3$-class and $10$-class problems), with anomaly detection: 1) datasets created after different numbers of observing years from a single survey (LSST mock data of $1$ and $10$ years of observations); 2) data from different surveys (SDSS and DECaLS); and 3) data from observing fields with different depths within one survey (wide field and Stripe 82 deep field of SDSS). For the first time, we demonstrate the successful use of domain adaptation between very discrepant observational datasets. \textit{DeepAstroUDA} is capable of bridging the gap between two astronomical surveys, increasing classification accuracy in both domains (up to $40\%$ on the unlabeled data), and making model performance consistent across datasets. Furthermore, our method also performs well as an anomaly detection algorithm and successfully clusters unknown class samples even in the unlabeled target dataset.
abstract_zh: 人工智能方法在提高大型天文数据集的工作质量和速度方面显示出巨大的希望，但这些方法的高度复杂性导致提取数据集特定的、不稳健的特征。因此，此类方法不能很好地推广到多个数据集。我们提出了一种通用的域适应方法，\textit{DeepAstroUDA}，作为克服这一挑战的方法。该算法执行半监督域适应，可以应用于具有不同数据分布和类重叠的数据集。非重叠类可以存在于两个数据集中的任何一个（标记的源域或未标记的目标域）中，并且该方法甚至可以在存在未知类的情况下使用。我们将我们的方法应用于不同复杂度的星系形态分类任务的三个示例（3美元级和10美元级问题），并进行异常检测：1）在一次调查的不同观测年数后创建的数据集（1美元和10美元观测年的LSST模拟数据）； 2) 来自不同调查的数据（SDSS 和 DECaLS）； 3）一次调查中不同深度观测场的数据（SDSS的宽场和Stripe 82深场）。我们第一次展示了在差异很大的观测数据集之间成功使用域适应。 \textit{DeepAstroUDA} 能够弥合两次天文调查之间的差距，提高两个领域的分类准确性（未标记数据高达 40\%$），并使模型性能在数据集中保持一致。此外，我们的方法作为异常检测算法也表现良好，即使在未标记的目标数据集中也能成功地聚类未知类样本。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DeepAstroUDA的半监督通用领域自适应方法，用于解决跨天文调查的星系形态分类和异常检测问题。
AI.motivation: 传统人工智能方法在处理天文学大数据时，因数据集特定特征提取导致跨数据集泛化能力差，亟需领域自适应技术提升模型鲁棒性。
AI.method: DeepAstroUDA采用半监督领域自适应框架，可处理不同数据分布、类别重叠及未知类别的场景，适用于多源天文数据的跨域迁移。
AI.result: 在三个不同复杂度的星系分类任务中，该方法使未标记数据分类准确率提升最高达40%，实现跨数据集性能一致性，并有效检测异常样本。
AI.conclusion: DeepAstroUDA成功弥合了天文调查间的数据鸿沟，显著提升分类准确率与模型一致性，同时具备良好的异常检测能力。

For the first time, we demonstrate the successful use of domain adaptation between very discrepant observational datasets. \textit{DeepAstroUDA} is capable of bridging the gap between two astronomical surveys, increasing classification accuracy in both domains (up to $40\%$ on the unlabeled data), and making model performance consistent across datasets. Furthermore, our method also performs well as an anomaly detection algorithm and successfully clusters unknown class samples even in the unlabeled target dataset. 人工智能方法在提高大型天文数据集的工作质量和速度方面显示出巨大的希望，但这些方法的高度复杂性导致提取数据集特定的、不稳健的特征。因此，此类方法不能很好地推广到多个数据集。我们提出了一种通用的域适应方法，\textit{DeepAstroUDA}，作为克服这一挑战的方法。该算法执行半监督域适应，可以应用于具有不同数据分布和类重叠的数据集。非重叠类可以存在于两个数据集中的任何一个（标记的源域或未标记的目标域）中，并且该方法甚至可以在存在未知类的情况下使用。我们将我们的方法应用于不同复杂度的星系形态分类任务的三个示例（3美元级和10美元级问题），并进行异常检测：1）在一次调查的不同观测年数后创建的数据集（1美元和10美元观测年的LSST模拟数据）； 2) 来自不同调查的数据（SDSS 和 DECaLS）； 3）一次调查中不同深度观测场的数据（SDSS的宽场和Stripe 82深场）。我们第一次展示了在差异很大的观测数据集之间成功使用域适应。 \textit{DeepAstroUDA} 能够弥合两次天文调查之间的差距，提高两个领域的分类准确性（未标记数据高达 40\%$），并使模型性能在数据集中保持一致。此外，我们的方法作为异常检测算法也表现良好，即使在未标记的目标数据集中也能成功地聚类未知类样本。 本文提出了一种名为DeepAstroUDA的半监督通用领域自适应方法，用于解决跨天文调查的星系形态分类和异常检测问题。 A. Ćiprijanović,A. Lewis,K. Pedro,S. Madireddy,B. Nord,G. N. Perdue,S. M. Wild astro-ph.GA,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Ji Liu,Max Bowman,Pranav Gokhale,Siddharth Dangwal,Jeffrey Larson,Frederic T. Chong,Paul D. Hovland
title: QContext: Context-Aware Decomposition for Quantum Gates
title_zh: QContext：量子门的上下文感知分解
abstract: In this paper we propose QContext, a new compiler structure that incorporates context-aware and topology-aware decompositions. Because of circuit equivalence rules and resynthesis, variants of a gate-decomposition template may exist. QContext exploits the circuit information and the hardware topology to select the gate variant that increases circuit optimization opportunities. We study the basis-gate-level context-aware decomposition for Toffoli gates and the native-gate-level context-aware decomposition for CNOT gates. Our experiments show that QContext reduces the number of gates as compared with the state-of-the-art approach, Orchestrated Trios.
summary: In this paper we propose QContext, a new compiler structure that incorporates context-aware and topology-aware decompositions. Because of circuit equivalence rules and resynthesis, variants of a gate-decomposition template may exist. QContext exploits the circuit information and the hardware topology to select the gate variant that increases circuit optimization opportunities. We study the basis-gate-level context-aware decomposition for Toffoli gates and the native-gate-level context-aware decomposition for CNOT gates. Our experiments show that QContext reduces the number of gates as compared with the state-of-the-art approach, Orchestrated Trios.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 QContext，一种新的编译器结构，它结合了上下文感知和拓扑感知分解。由于电路等效规则和重新综合，可能存在门分解模板的变体。 QContext 利用电路信息和硬件拓扑来选择可增加电路优化机会的门变体。我们研究了 Toffoli 门的基础门级上下文感知分解和 CNOT 门的本机门级上下文感知分解。我们的实验表明，与最先进的方法 Orchestrated Trios 相比，QContext 减少了门的数量。

AI.tldr: 提出一种名为QContext的编译器结构，通过上下文和拓扑感知分解优化量子门分解，减少电路中的门数量。
AI.motivation: 现有量子门分解方法未能有效利用电路上下文和硬件拓扑信息，导致优化机会受限，需更智能的分解策略。
AI.method: 结合电路信息与硬件拓扑特性，动态选择最优门分解变体，分别针对Toffoli门和CNOT门进行基础级与原生级分解优化。
AI.result: 实验表明QContext相比现有方法显著减少了量子电路中的门数量。
AI.conclusion: QContext通过上下文感知分解有效提升量子电路优化效率，为量子编译器设计提供新思路。

authors_text: Lasse Peters,Vicenç Rubies-Royo,Claire J. Tomlin,Laura Ferranti,Javier Alonso-Mora,Cyrill Stachniss,David Fridovich-Keil
title: Online and Offline Learning of Player Objectives from Partial Observations in Dynamic Games
title_zh: 从动态游戏中的部分观察中在线和离线学习玩家目标
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2106.03611
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2106.03611
abstract: Robots deployed to the real world must be able to interact with other agents in their environment. Dynamic game theory provides a powerful mathematical framework for modeling scenarios in which agents have individual objectives and interactions evolve over time. However, a key limitation of such techniques is that they require a-priori knowledge of all players' objectives. In this work, we address this issue by proposing a novel method for learning players' objectives in continuous dynamic games from noise-corrupted, partial state observations. Our approach learns objectives by coupling the estimation of unknown cost parameters of each player with inference of unobserved states and inputs through Nash equilibrium constraints. By coupling past state estimates with future state predictions, our approach is amenable to simultaneous online learning and prediction in receding horizon fashion. We demonstrate our method in several simulated traffic scenarios in which we recover players' preferences for, e.g., desired travel speed and collision-avoidance behavior. Results show that our method reliably estimates game-theoretic models from noise-corrupted data that closely matches ground-truth objectives, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art approaches.
summary: Robots deployed to the real world must be able to interact with other agents in their environment. Dynamic game theory provides a powerful mathematical framework for modeling scenarios in which agents have individual objectives and interactions evolve over time. However, a key limitation of such techniques is that they require a-priori knowledge of all players' objectives. In this work, we address this issue by proposing a novel method for learning players' objectives in continuous dynamic games from noise-corrupted, partial state observations. Our approach learns objectives by coupling the estimation of unknown cost parameters of each player with inference of unobserved states and inputs through Nash equilibrium constraints. By coupling past state estimates with future state predictions, our approach is amenable to simultaneous online learning and prediction in receding horizon fashion. We demonstrate our method in several simulated traffic scenarios in which we recover players' preferences for, e.g., desired travel speed and collision-avoidance behavior. Results show that our method reliably estimates game-theoretic models from noise-corrupted data that closely matches ground-truth objectives, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art approaches.
abstract_zh: 部署到现实世界的机器人必须能够与环境中的其他代理进行交互。动态博弈论为建模场景提供了强大的数学框架，在这些场景中，代理具有单独的目标，并且交互随着时间的推移而演变。然而，此类技术的一个关键限制是它们需要对所有参与者的目标有先验知识。在这项工作中，我们通过提出一种新方法来解决这个问题，该方法可以从噪声破坏的部分状态观察中学习连续动态游戏中玩家的目标。我们的方法通过将每个参与者的未知成本参数的估计与通过纳什均衡约束对未观察状态和输入的推断结合起来来学习目标。通过将过去的状态估计与未来的状态预测相结合，我们的方法适合以后退方式同时进行在线学习和预测。我们在几个模拟交通场景中展示了我们的方法，在这些场景中我们恢复了玩家的偏好，例如所需的行驶速度和防撞行为。结果表明，我们的方法可以根据噪声损坏的数据可靠地估计博弈论模型，这些模型与地面实况目标非常匹配，始终优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从部分观测数据中学习动态博弈中玩家目标的新方法，无需预先知道玩家目标。
AI.motivation: 传统动态博弈方法需要预先知道所有玩家的目标，但在现实场景中难以获取这些先验知识，限制了其应用。
AI.method: 通过结合纳什均衡约束，同时估计未知成本参数和未观测状态，利用在线与离线学习框架实现滚动时域的预测与学习。
AI.result: 在模拟交通场景中成功恢复玩家对速度偏好和避撞行为的目标，且估计结果优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了部分观测下动态博弈目标学习问题，验证了其在噪声数据中的鲁棒性与优越性。

authors_text: Huy Tu,Tim Menzies
title: Less, but Stronger: On the Value of Strong Heuristics in Semi-supervised Learning for Software Analytics
title_zh: 更少，但更强：论强启发式在软件分析半监督学习中的价值
abstract: In many domains, there are many examples and far fewer labels for those examples; e.g. we may have access to millions of lines of source code, but access to only a handful of warnings about that code. In those domains, semi-supervised learners (SSL) can extrapolate labels from a small number of examples to the rest of the data. Standard SSL algorithms use ``weak'' knowledge (i.e. those not based on specific SE knowledge) such as (e.g.) co-train two learners and use good labels from one to train the other. Another approach of SSL in software analytics is potentially use ``strong'' knowledge that use SE knowledge. For example, an often-used heuristic in SE is that unusually large artifacts contain undesired properties (e.g. more bugs). This paper argues that such ``strong'' algorithms perform better than those standard, weaker, SSL algorithms. We show this by learning models from labels generated using weak SSL or our ``stronger'' FRUGAL algorithm. In four domains (distinguishing security-related bug reports; mitigating bias in decision-making; predicting issue close time; and (reducing false alarms in static code warnings), FRUGAL required only 2.5% of the data to be labeled yet out-performed standard semi-supervised learners that relied on (e.g.) some domain-independent graph theory concepts. Hence, for future work, we strongly recommend the use of strong heuristics for semi-supervised learning for SE applications. To better support other researchers, our scripts and data are on-line at https://github.com/HuyTu7/FRUGAL.
summary: In many domains, there are many examples and far fewer labels for those examples; e.g. we may have access to millions of lines of source code, but access to only a handful of warnings about that code. In those domains, semi-supervised learners (SSL) can extrapolate labels from a small number of examples to the rest of the data. Standard SSL algorithms use ``weak'' knowledge (i.e. those not based on specific SE knowledge) such as (e.g.) co-train two learners and use good labels from one to train the other. Another approach of SSL in software analytics is potentially use ``strong'' knowledge that use SE knowledge. For example, an often-used heuristic in SE is that unusually large artifacts contain undesired properties (e.g. more bugs). This paper argues that such ``strong'' algorithms perform better than those standard, weaker, SSL algorithms. We show this by learning models from labels generated using weak SSL or our ``stronger'' FRUGAL algorithm. In four domains (distinguishing security-related bug reports; mitigating bias in decision-making; predicting issue close time; and (reducing false alarms in static code warnings), FRUGAL required only 2.5% of the data to be labeled yet out-performed standard semi-supervised learners that relied on (e.g.) some domain-independent graph theory concepts. Hence, for future work, we strongly recommend the use of strong heuristics for semi-supervised learning for SE applications. To better support other researchers, our scripts and data are on-line at https://github.com/HuyTu7/FRUGAL.
abstract_zh: 在许多领域，有很多例子，但这些例子的标签却少得多；例如我们可能可以访问数百万行源代码，但只能访问有关该代码的少数警告。在这些领域中，半监督学习器 (SSL) 可以将标签从少量示例推断到其余数据。标准 SSL 算法使用“弱”知识（即那些不基于特定 SE 知识的知识），例如共同训练两个学习者并使用一个学习者的良好标签来训练另一个学习者。 SSL 在软件分析中的另一种方法是可能使用使用 SE 知识的“强”知识。例如，SE 中经常使用的启发法是异常大的工件包含不需要的属性（例如更多错误）。本文认为，这种“强”算法比那些标准的、较弱的 SSL 算法性能更好。我们通过从使用弱 SSL 或“更强”的 FRUGAL 算法生成的标签中学习模型来展示这一点。在四个领域（区分与安全相关的错误报告；减轻决策中的偏差；预测问题关闭时间；以及（减少静态代码警告中的误报）），FRUGAL 仅需要标记 2.5％ 的数据，但其表现优于依赖于（例如）一些与领域无关的图论概念的标准半监督学习器。因此，对于未来的工作，我们强烈建议使用强启发式进行半监督学习为了更好地支持其他研究人员，我们的脚本和数据位于 https://github.com/HuyTu7/FRUGAL 上。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了在软件分析中使用强启发式方法的半监督学习（SSL）的价值，并提出了一种名为FRUGAL的算法。
AI.motivation: 在软件工程领域，存在大量未标记数据但标记数据极少的问题，传统SSL方法依赖弱知识（如图论）效果有限，需探索更有效的强启发式方法。
AI.method: 提出FRUGAL算法，利用软件工程领域知识（如异常大代码片段可能包含缺陷）作为强启发式规则，结合半监督学习框架进行模型训练。
AI.result: 在四个软件分析任务中（安全漏洞识别、决策偏见缓解、问题关闭时间预测、静态警告误报减少），FRUGAL仅需2.5%标记数据即可超越传统SSL方法。
AI.conclusion: 强启发式方法在软件工程的半监督学习中显著优于传统弱知识方法，建议未来研究优先采用领域知识驱动的SSL策略。

search_text: Less, but Stronger: On the Value of Strong Heuristics in Semi-supervised Learning for Software Analytics 更少，但更强：论强启发式在软件分析半监督学习中的价值 In many domains, there are many examples and far fewer labels for those examples; e.g. we may have access to millions of lines of source code, but access to only a handful of warnings about that code. In those domains, semi-supervised learners (SSL) can extrapolate labels from a small number of examples to the rest of the data. Standard SSL algorithms use ``weak'' knowledge (i.e. those not based on specific SE knowledge) such as (e.g.) co-train two learners and use good labels from one to train the other. Another approach of SSL in software analytics is potentially use ``strong'' knowledge that use SE knowledge. For example, an often-used heuristic in SE is that unusually large artifacts contain undesired properties (e.g. more bugs). This paper argues that such ``strong'' algorithms perform better than those standard, weaker, SSL algorithms. We show this by learning models from labels generated using weak SSL or our ``stronger'' FRUGAL algorithm. In four domains (distinguishing security-related bug reports; mitigating bias in decision-making; predicting issue close time; and (reducing false alarms in static code warnings), FRUGAL required only 2.5% of the data to be labeled yet out-performed standard semi-supervised learners that relied on (e.g.) some domain-independent graph theory concepts. Hence, for future work, we strongly recommend the use of strong heuristics for semi-supervised learning for SE applications. To better support other researchers, our scripts and data are on-line at https://github.com/HuyTu7/FRUGAL. 在许多领域，有很多例子，但这些例子的标签却少得多；例如我们可能可以访问数百万行源代码，但只能访问有关该代码的少数警告。在这些领域中，半监督学习器 (SSL) 可以将标签从少量示例推断到其余数据。标准 SSL 算法使用“弱”知识（即那些不基于特定 SE 知识的知识），例如共同训练两个学习者并使用一个学习者的良好标签来训练另一个学习者。 SSL 在软件分析中的另一种方法是可能使用使用 SE 知识的“强”知识。例如，SE 中经常使用的启发法是异常大的工件包含不需要的属性（例如更多错误）。本文认为，这种“强”算法比那些标准的、较弱的 SSL 算法性能更好。我们通过从使用弱 SSL 或“更强”的 FRUGAL 算法生成的标签中学习模型来展示这一点。在四个领域（区分与安全相关的错误报告；减轻决策中的偏差；预测问题关闭时间；以及（减少静态代码警告中的误报）），FRUGAL 仅需要标记 2.5％ 的数据，但其表现优于依赖于（例如）一些与领域无关的图论概念的标准半监督学习器。因此，对于未来的工作，我们强烈建议使用强启发式进行半监督学习为了更好地支持其他研究人员，我们的脚本和数据位于 https://github.com/HuyTu7/FRUGAL 上。 本文探讨了在软件分析中使用强启发式方法的半监督学习（SSL）的价值，并提出了一种名为FRUGAL的算法。 Huy Tu,Tim Menzies cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Sedat Ozer,Enes Ilhan,Mehmet Akif Ozkanoglu,Hakan Ali Cirpan
title: Offloading Deep Learning Powered Vision Tasks from UAV to 5G Edge Server with Denoising
title_zh: 通过去噪将深度学习驱动的视觉任务从无人机卸载到 5G 边缘服务器
comment: This paper is accepted for publication at IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
comments: This paper is accepted for publication at IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Offloading computationally heavy tasks from an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) to a remote server helps improve the battery life and can help reduce resource requirements. Deep learning based state-of-the-art computer vision tasks, such as object segmentation and object detection, are computationally heavy algorithms, requiring large memory and computing power. Many UAVs are using (pretrained) off-the-shelf versions of such algorithms. Offloading such power-hungry algorithms to a remote server could help UAVs save power significantly. However, deep learning based algorithms are susceptible to noise, and a wireless communication system, by its nature, introduces noise to the original signal. When the signal represents an image, noise affects the image. There has not been much work studying the effect of the noise introduced by the communication system on pretrained deep networks. In this work, we first analyze how reliable it is to offload deep learning based computer vision tasks (including both object segmentation and detection) by focusing on the effect of various parameters of a 5G wireless communication system on the transmitted image and demonstrate how the introduced noise of the used 5G wireless communication system reduces the performance of the offloaded deep learning task. Then solutions are introduced to eliminate (or reduce) the negative effect of the noise. The proposed framework starts with introducing many classical techniques as alternative solutions first, and then introduces a novel deep learning based solution to denoise the given noisy input image. The performance of various denoising algorithms on offloading both object segmentation and object detection tasks are compared. Our proposed deep transformer-based denoiser algorithm (NR-Net) yields the state-of-the-art results on reducing the negative effect of the noise in our experiments.
summary: Offloading computationally heavy tasks from an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) to a remote server helps improve the battery life and can help reduce resource requirements. Deep learning based state-of-the-art computer vision tasks, such as object segmentation and object detection, are computationally heavy algorithms, requiring large memory and computing power. Many UAVs are using (pretrained) off-the-shelf versions of such algorithms. Offloading such power-hungry algorithms to a remote server could help UAVs save power significantly. However, deep learning based algorithms are susceptible to noise, and a wireless communication system, by its nature, introduces noise to the original signal. When the signal represents an image, noise affects the image. There has not been much work studying the effect of the noise introduced by the communication system on pretrained deep networks. In this work, we first analyze how reliable it is to offload deep learning based computer vision tasks (including both object segmentation and detection) by focusing on the effect of various parameters of a 5G wireless communication system on the transmitted image and demonstrate how the introduced noise of the used 5G wireless communication system reduces the performance of the offloaded deep learning task. Then solutions are introduced to eliminate (or reduce) the negative effect of the noise. The proposed framework starts with introducing many classical techniques as alternative solutions first, and then introduces a novel deep learning based solution to denoise the given noisy input image. The performance of various denoising algorithms on offloading both object segmentation and object detection tasks are compared. Our proposed deep transformer-based denoiser algorithm (NR-Net) yields the state-of-the-art results on reducing the negative effect of the noise in our experiments.
abstract_zh: 将计算繁重的任务从无人机 (UAV) 卸载到远程服务器有助于延长电池寿命，并有助于减少资源需求。基于深度学习的最先进的计算机视觉任务（例如对象分割和对象检测）是计算量大的算法，需要大量内存和计算能力。许多无人机正在使用此类算法的（预训练的）现成版本。将这种耗电的算法卸载到远程服务器可以帮助无人机显着节省电力。然而，基于深度学习的算法容易受到噪声的影响，而无线通信系统本质上会向原始信号引入噪声。当信号代表图像时，噪声会影响图像。目前还没有太多的工作来研究通信系统引入的噪声对预训练深度网络的影响。在这项工作中，我们首先通过关注 5G 无线通信系统的各种参数对传输图像的影响来分析卸载基于深度学习的计算机视觉任务（包括对象分割和检测）的可靠性，并演示所使用的 5G 无线通信系统引入的噪声如何降低卸载的深度学习任务的性能。然后引入解决方案来消除（或减少）噪声的负面影响。所提出的框架首先引入许多经典技术作为替代解决方案，然后引入一种新颖的基于深度学习的解决方案来对给定的噪声输入图像进行去噪。比较了各种去噪算法在减轻对象分割和对象检测任务方面的性能。我们提出的基于深度变压器的降噪算法（NR-Net）在减少实验中噪声的负面影响方面产生了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究将深度学习视觉任务从无人机卸载到5G边缘服务器时的噪声影响及去噪方法。
AI.motivation: 无人机计算资源有限，卸载深度学习任务可节省电量，但无线通信噪声会显著影响图像质量及模型性能，现有研究缺乏对此问题的系统分析。
AI.method: 分析5G通信参数对图像传输噪声的影响，对比经典去噪算法，提出基于深度变压器的NR-Net算法进行图像去噪。
AI.result: 实验表明，所提出的NR-Net算法在目标分割和检测任务中均能有效抑制通信噪声带来的性能下降，达到最先进水平。
AI.conclusion: 通信噪声会显著降低卸载任务性能，而提出的深度学习去噪框架可有效缓解该问题，为无人机边缘计算提供可行方案。

authors_text: Dongjie Wang,Zhengzhang Chen,Jingchao Ni,Liang Tong,Zheng Wang,Yanjie Fu,Haifeng Chen
title: Hierarchical Graph Neural Networks for Causal Discovery and Root Cause Localization
title_zh: 用于因果发现和根本原因定位的分层图神经网络
abstract: In this paper, we propose REASON, a novel framework that enables the automatic discovery of both intra-level (i.e., within-network) and inter-level (i.e., across-network) causal relationships for root cause localization. REASON consists of Topological Causal Discovery and Individual Causal Discovery. The Topological Causal Discovery component aims to model the fault propagation in order to trace back to the root causes. To achieve this, we propose novel hierarchical graph neural networks to construct interdependent causal networks by modeling both intra-level and inter-level non-linear causal relations. Based on the learned interdependent causal networks, we then leverage random walks with restarts to model the network propagation of a system fault. The Individual Causal Discovery component focuses on capturing abrupt change patterns of a single system entity. This component examines the temporal patterns of each entity's metric data (i.e., time series), and estimates its likelihood of being a root cause based on the Extreme Value theory. Combining the topological and individual causal scores, the top K system entities are identified as root causes. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets with case studies demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework.
summary: In this paper, we propose REASON, a novel framework that enables the automatic discovery of both intra-level (i.e., within-network) and inter-level (i.e., across-network) causal relationships for root cause localization. REASON consists of Topological Causal Discovery and Individual Causal Discovery. The Topological Causal Discovery component aims to model the fault propagation in order to trace back to the root causes. To achieve this, we propose novel hierarchical graph neural networks to construct interdependent causal networks by modeling both intra-level and inter-level non-linear causal relations. Based on the learned interdependent causal networks, we then leverage random walks with restarts to model the network propagation of a system fault. The Individual Causal Discovery component focuses on capturing abrupt change patterns of a single system entity. This component examines the temporal patterns of each entity's metric data (i.e., time series), and estimates its likelihood of being a root cause based on the Extreme Value theory. Combining the topological and individual causal scores, the top K system entities are identified as root causes. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets with case studies demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 REASON，这是一种新颖的框架，可以自动发现层内（即网络内）和层间（即跨网络）因果关系以进行根本原因定位。 REASON 由拓扑因果发现和个体因果发现组成。拓扑因果发现组件旨在对故障传播进行建模，以便追溯到根本原因。为了实现这一目标，我们提出了新颖的分层图神经网络，通过建模层内和层间非线性因果关系来构建相互依赖的因果网络。基于学习到的相互依赖的因果网络，我们然后利用带有重启的随机游走来模拟系统故障的网络传播。个体因果发现组件专注于捕获单个系统实体的突然变化模式。该组件检查每个实体的度量数据（即时间序列）的时间模式，并根据极值理论估计其成为根本原因的可能性。结合拓扑和个体因果分数，前 K 个系统实体被识别为根本原因。对三个真实世界数据集的广泛实验和案例研究证明了所提出框架的有效性和优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出REASON框架，通过分层图神经网络自动发现系统内的层级间和跨网络因果关系，实现根因定位。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以有效捕捉复杂系统中层级内和层级间的非线性因果关系，导致根因定位效率低下。
AI.method: REASON框架包含拓扑因果发现和个体因果发现两部分：前者利用分层图神经网络建模层级间因果关系，并通过重启随机游走模拟故障传播；后者基于极值理论分析实体时间序列的突变模式。
AI.result: 在三个真实数据集上的实验表明，REASON在根因定位任务中优于现有方法，具有更高的准确性和鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: REASON通过融合拓扑结构与时间序列特征，有效解决了复杂系统中跨层级因果关系建模难题，为根因分析提供了新思路。
